content: The intrinsic reward signals from curiosity-driven RL methods (ICM/RND) can be repurposed as a dynamic memory pruning criterion for state-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems, where only unexpected, high-intrinsic-reward experiences are retained for future planning. This replaces fixed-size or recency-based pruning with a curiosity-aligned retention policy that prioritizes information that drives future exploration.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
